severity_rationale: Source-confirmed cross-origin postMessage acceptance in 4 production survey listeners on you.23andme.com (cogstate, hearing, cog_game, cog_game_stream). Attacker-controlled page can postMessage to the victim tab and (1) force survey-complete state (research data integrity), (2) inject fabricated hearing-test result, (3) mutate iframe DOM state. Integrity impact on regulated research data. Primary exploit confirmed in handler-replica test; full live PoC deferred due to survey-enrolment requirement.
validation_method: Source-code review + byte-for-byte handler replicas tested in dev-browser with origin='https://evil.example.com'.
endpoint: you.23andme.com survey iframes (cogstate, hearing, cog_game, cog_game_stream)
response_summary: Four production listeners accept cross-origin postMessage with NO event.origin validation. Handler replicas fired save/error/resize actions from evil.example.com origin.
impact: Research-study data integrity violation (fabricated survey results), iframe state manipulation, potential chain to stored-XSS via research-admin review surfaces (speculation — not demonstrated).
chain: postmessage + xss chain candidate: if any admin/researcher-review UI renders fabricated survey payload unescaped, stored XSS on research admin surface. Not demonstrated — would require access to researcher view.
remediation: Add event.origin allowlist check (e.g., event.origin === 'https://you.23andme.com') at the top of each listener before processing. Consider postMessage channel with MessageChannel + transferable ports for iframe->parent trust boundary.

reportability_audit.Q3_impact_demonstrated: PASS — attacker origin accepted, action fired
